text: Pulsed field gradient (PFG) NMR experiments were carried out on solutions of different aqueous mixtures of choline chloride-based deep eutectic solvents (DESs). The mixtures investigated do not behave as a homogeneous simple liquid. Through self-diffusion of the different species present in the mixtures, the changes of the intermolecular interactions in the mixtures induced by the addition of water are studied. They imply the choline cation, the hydrogen bond donor and the water molecules. The self-diffusion coefficients of the hydroxyl protons in the mixtures Ethaline and Reline are different from those of their parent molecules. This can be explained by a strong chemical exchange implying changes in the species present in solution as suggested by the analysis of 1H NMR spectra of Reline showing the presence of significant amounts of the species corresponding to ammonium hydroxide. The self-diffusion coefficient of water in DES-water mixtures is much larger than that expected for a homogeneous mixture, indicating the presence of fast mass transport processes. The mixtures investigated are heterogeneous at the microscopic scale: the high affinity of DES components towards water suggests the presence of water-rich domains in the mixtures contributing to the fast mass transport processes occurring in the mixtures.
